name: Polish Bigos (Sauerkraut Stew)
recipeInstructions:
1. Brown the beef, pork, and spareribs in a large heavy pot.
2. Transfer the browned meats and smoked butt with 1 cup of water to a separate, covered pan. Simmer until tender: about half an hour for the pork chops, one hour for the butt, and one and a half to two hours for beef and two hours for spareribs.
3. Drain all the fat from the first pot and add sauerkraut and 1 cup of water to it. Chop the cabbage finely and mix into the sauerkraut. Cover and cook until the cabbage is tender, approximately 30 minutes. Keep the pot on a very low simmer after removing the lid.
4. In another pan, fry bacon until crisp, then crumble it into the sauerkraut mixture. Remove most of the bacon fat and add fried onions and flour to the pan, cooking just until they brown. Mix this into the sauerkraut.
5. Once meats are tender in the second pan, remove them, trim the fat and bones from beef, and cut into small pieces. Add these pieces to the sauerkraut mixture along with any meat juices skimmed off.
6. Remove the skin from kielbasa and slice it. Add these slices to the sauerkraut mixture along with the tomatoes. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
7. Bring the mixture to a boil, then simmer for five minutes before serving hot.
